summary: reminders create + reminders cancel now skip the userdata.write call when --dry-run is set; the prior code only decorated the response envelope after the write had already executed.
reason: Greptile P1 round-3 on PR #595: flags.dryRun was checked AFTER c.Post(path, body), so the snooze (create) or unreminder (cancel) write had already fired by the time the dry-run flag was honored. Gated the userdata.write call on !flags.dryRun and synthesize a {dry_run, path, method, planned_body, would_send_write} preview when dry-run is set. The userdata.read call earlier in reminders create stays live because it is a read-shaped POST and lets the preview include the real messageIds the write would have used.
validated_outcome: go build / go vet / 566 tests / 17 packages green; verify-skill clean.
